task_completion.description: Open the bcWebCam settings or configuration window
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam application is running and its settings/preferences dialog box is opened on the screen.
A checkbox or toggle labeled 'Always on top' (or similar) is checked in the bcWebCam settings window.
An opacity slider, dropdown, or text input field in the settings is adjusted to 60% (or 0.6).

task_completion.description: Remove the terminating character after scans
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'terminating character', 'postfix', or 'send keys' setting is cleared, emptied, or set to 'None' (removing the previous 'Enter' value).

task_completion.description: Set a 1-second duplicate prevention grace period
task_completion.visual_evidence: A setting related to 'duplicate prevention', 'grace period', or 'ignore duplicates' is set to 1 second (or 1000 ms).
The agent clicks 'OK', 'Save', or 'Apply' in the settings window to confirm the changes, and the settings window closes.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to configure the application, not terminal commands or text editors.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the bcWebCam GUI. There should be no text editors (like Notepad) or terminal windows open modifying an .ini configuration file directly.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with the application's input fields and checkboxes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for intermediate states such as dropdowns being opened, text being deleted/typed in input fields, or the mouse cursor hovering over checkboxes.

integrity.description: The agent did not write or execute a script to bypass manual configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: No IDEs, code editors, or command prompts executing Python/Bash scripts to patch the application's settings.
